source.notes: Imported from Azure DevOps work item 6447

observedSteps: User Story

As a user, I want to authenticate from the Home page and access authenticated Home page feature actions, so that I can navigate to Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell workflows from the Home page.
Application Context

WashTab is a single page application (SPA). The Home page initially loads in guest state. Authentication is initiated from the existing Home page and completed through the existing Login page. After successful login, the application returns to the same shared Home page in authenticated state.
Authenticated feature actions remain part of the shared Home page experience and must reuse the existing Home page resource/POM rather than introducing a separate authenticated Home page resource.
Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell workflows are accessed through authenticated Home page navigation actions. Navigation validation is based on URL transition behavior.
Entry Conditions
User starts on the Home page in guest state.
Existing approved Home page resource/POM is available for reuse.
Existing approved Login page resource/POM is available for reuse.
Approved authentication credentials from the existing Login workflow are available for reuse.
Workflow Scope
This workflow covers:
Navigation from guest Home page to Login page.
Authentication from Login page back to authenticated Home state.
Validation of authenticated Home feature-button visibility and access.
Navigation from authenticated Home page to Vehicles On Site workflow.
Navigation from authenticated Home page to Damage Claim workflow.
Navigation from authenticated Home page to Presell workflow.
URL transition validation for authenticated feature navigation.
Reuse and extension of the shared Home page resource/POM.
Primary Navigation Journey
User opens the Home page in guest state.
User selects the Person/Profile button.
Application transitions to the Login page.
User enters approved username and password credentials.
User selects the Login button.
Application returns to the Home page in authenticated state.
Authenticated Home feature buttons become visible and actionable.
User selects the Vehicles On Site button.
Application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
User returns to the authenticated Home page.
User selects the Damage Claim button.
Application navigates to a URL containing damage-claim.
User selects the Presell button.
Application navigates to a URL containing presell?data.
Approved Test Data
Reuse the approved username and password from the existing Login workflow.
No new authentication data is introduced in this workflow.
Approved authentication data must be reused consistently across manual and automation assets.
Page / Component Elements
Home Page Elements:
Person/Profile button
Vehicles On Site button
Damage Claim button
Presell button
Shared authenticated Home shell controls
Login Page Elements:
Username field
Password field
Login button
Ownership and Reuse:
Guest and authenticated Home navigation controls remain owned by the existing Home page resource/POM.
Login input and submission controls remain owned by the existing Login page resource/POM.
Destination workflows should reuse their existing workflow resources if already defined.
Feature navigation controls must not be duplicated into unrelated resources.
Business Rules
Selecting the Person/Profile button from guest Home must open the Login page.
Successful login must return the application to the Home page in authenticated state.
Vehicles On Site navigation must transition the application to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
Damage Claim navigation must transition the application to a URL containing damage-claim.
Presell navigation must transition the application to a URL containing presell?data.
Feature navigation actions are available only from the authenticated Home state.
Authenticated Home functionality remains part of the shared Home page implementation.
Existing Home and Login resource ownership boundaries must remain preserved.
Validation Expectations
Person/Profile button is visible and actionable on the guest Home page.
Username field, Password field, and Login button are visible and actionable on the Login page.
After successful login, Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell buttons are visible and actionable on the authenticated Home page.
Selecting Vehicles On Site transitions the application to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
Selecting Damage Claim transitions the application to a URL containing damage-claim.
Selecting Presell transitions the application to a URL containing presell?data.
Authenticated Home navigation behavior reuses the existing Home page resource structure.
Transition Expectations
Workflow begins on the Home page in guest state.
Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application to the Login page.
Entering approved credentials and selecting Login transitions the application back to the Home page in authenticated state.
Authenticated Home navigation remains within the shared SPA application structure.
Resource Reuse Guidance
Reuse the existing Home page resource/POM for guest Home entry, authenticated Home navigation actions, and Home page validations.
Reuse the existing Login page resource/POM for username entry, password entry, and login submission actions.
Extend the existing Home page resource/POM only when additional authenticated feature elements are required.
Do not create a separate authenticated Home page resource.
Reuse existing downstream workflow resources for Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell destinations when available.
Avoid duplicate ownership of shared Home and Login navigation controls.
Downstream Automation Guidance
Treat authenticated feature navigation as an extension of the shared Home page workflow.
Reuse existing Login automation steps and approved authentication data.
Generate automation coverage for:
Guest Home to Login navigation
Login completion and authenticated Home return flow
Authenticated Home feature-button visibility
Authenticated feature URL navigation validation
Validate the following exact URL fragments:
vehicle-onsite
damage-claim
presell?data
Maintain ownership boundaries between Home, Login, and downstream workflow resources.
Generate manual and automation assets using the shared Home page resource/POM.
Acceptance Criteria
Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user selects the Person/Profile button, then the application navigates to the Login page.
Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters approved username and password and selects the Login button, then the application returns to the Home page in authenticated state.
Given the authenticated Home page is displayed, when the page loads in authenticated state, then the Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell buttons are visible and actionable.
Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Vehicles On Site button, then the application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Damage Claim button, then the application navigates to a URL containing damage-claim.
Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Presell button, then the application navigates to a URL containing presell?data.

Given authenticated Home navigation resources are generated, when shared resources are reused, then Home navigation actions reuse the existing Home page resource/POM and login actions reuse the existing Login page resource/POM.

externalContext.title: Home Feature- Authenticated State
externalContext.description:
1. User Story
 
 As a user, I want to authenticate from the Home page and access authenticated Home page feature actions, so that I can navigate to Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell workflows from the Home page.
 
 2. Application Context
 
 WashTab is a single page application (SPA). The Home page initially loads in guest state. Authentication is initiated from the existing Home page and completed through the existing Login page. After successful login, the application returns to the same shared Home page in authenticated state.
 
 Authenticated feature actions remain part of the shared Home page experience and must reuse the existing Home page resource/POM rather than introducing a separate authenticated Home page resource.
 
 Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell workflows are accessed through authenticated Home page navigation actions. Navigation validation is based on URL transition behavior.
 
 3. Entry Conditions
 
 - User starts on the Home page in guest state.
 - Existing approved Home page resource/POM is available for reuse.
 - Existing approved Login page resource/POM is available for reuse.
 - Approved authentication credentials from the existing Login workflow are available for reuse.
 
 4. Workflow Scope
 
 This workflow covers:
 - Navigation from guest Home page to Login page.
 - Authentication from Login page back to authenticated Home state.
 - Validation of authenticated Home feature-button visibility and access.
 - Navigation from authenticated Home page to Vehicles On Site workflow.
 - Navigation from authenticated Home page to Damage Claim workflow.
 - Navigation from authenticated Home page to Presell workflow.
 - URL transition validation for authenticated feature navigation.
 - Reuse and extension of the shared Home page resource/POM.
 
 5. Primary Navigation Journey
 
 - User opens the Home page in guest state.
 - User selects the Person/Profile button.
 - Application transitions to the Login page.
 - User enters approved username and password credentials.
 - User selects the Login button.
 - Application returns to the Home page in authenticated state.
 - Authenticated Home feature buttons become visible and actionable.
 - User selects the Vehicles On Site button.
 - Application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
 - User returns to the authenticated Home page.
 - User selects the Damage Claim button.
 - Application navigates to a URL containing damage-claim.
 - User returns to the authenticated Home page.
 - User selects the Presell button.
 - Application navigates to a URL containing presell?data.
 
 6. Approved Test Data
 
 - Reuse the approved username and password from the existing Login workflow.
 - No new authentication data is introduced in this workflow.
 - Approved authentication data must be reused consistently across manual and automation assets.
 
 7. Page / Component Elements
 
 Home Page Elements:
 - Person/Profile button
 - Vehicles On Site button
 - Damage Claim button
 - Presell button
 - Shared authenticated Home shell controls
 
 Login Page Elements:
 - Username field
 - Password field
 - Login button
 
 Ownership and Reuse:
 - Guest and authenticated Home navigation controls remain owned by the existing Home page resource/POM.
 - Login input and submission controls remain owned by the existing Login page resource/POM.
 - Destination workflows should reuse their existing workflow resources if already defined.
 - Feature navigation controls must not be duplicated into unrelated resources.
 
 8. Business Rules
 
 - Selecting the Person/Profile button from guest Home must open the Login page.
 - Successful login must return the application to the Home page in authenticated state.
 - Vehicles On Site navigation must transition the application to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
 - Damage Claim navigation must transition the application to a URL containing damage-claim.
 - Presell navigation must transition the application to a URL containing presell?data.
 - Feature navigation actions are available only from the authenticated Home state.
 - Authenticated Home functionality remains part of the shared Home page implementation.
 - Existing Home and Login resource ownership boundaries must remain preserved.
 
 9. Validation Expectations
 
 - Person/Profile button is visible and actionable on the guest Home page.
 - Username field, Password field, and Login button are visible and actionable on the Login page.
 - After successful login, Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell buttons are visible and actionable on the authenticated Home page.
 - Selecting Vehicles On Site transitions the application to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
 - Selecting Damage Claim transitions the application to a URL containing damage-claim.
 - Selecting Presell transitions the application to a URL containing presell?data.
 - Authenticated Home navigation behavior reuses the existing Home page resource structure.
 
 10. Transition Expectations
 
 - Workflow begins on the Home page in guest state.
 - Selecting the Person/Profile button transitions the application to the Login page.
 - Entering approved credentials and selecting Login transitions the application back to the Home page in authenticated state.
 - Selecting Vehicles On Site transitions the application to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
 - Selecting Damage Claim transitions the application to a URL containing damage-claim.
 - Selecting Presell transitions the application to a URL containing presell?data.
 - Authenticated Home navigation remains within the shared SPA application structure.
 
 11. Resource Reuse Guidance
 
 - Reuse the existing Home page resource/POM for guest Home entry, authenticated Home navigation actions, and Home page validations.
 - Reuse the existing Login page resource/POM for username entry, password entry, and login submission actions.
 - Extend the existing Home page resource/POM only when additional authenticated feature elements are required.
 - Do not create a separate authenticated Home page resource.
 - Reuse existing downstream workflow resources for Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell destinations when available.
 - Avoid duplicate ownership of shared Home and Login navigation controls.
 
 12. Downstream Automation Guidance
 
 - Treat authenticated feature navigation as an extension of the shared Home page workflow.
 - Reuse existing Login automation steps and approved authentication data.
 - Generate automation coverage for:
 - Guest Home to Login navigation
 - Login completion and authenticated Home return flow
 - Authenticated Home feature-button visibility
 - Authenticated feature URL navigation validation
 - Validate the following exact URL fragments:
 - vehicle-onsite
 - damage-claim
 - presell?data
 - Maintain ownership boundaries between Home, Login, and downstream workflow resources.
 - Generate manual and automation assets using the shared Home page resource/POM.
 
 13. Acceptance Criteria
 
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user selects the Person/Profile button, then the application navigates to the Login page.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters approved username and password and selects the Login button, then the application returns to the Home page in authenticated state.
 - Given the authenticated Home page is displayed, when the page loads in authenticated state, then the Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell buttons are visible and actionable.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Vehicles On Site button, then the application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Damage Claim button, then the application navigates to a URL containing damage-claim.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Presell button, then the application navigates to a URL containing presell?data.
 - Given authenticated Home navigation resources are generated, when shared resources are reused, then Home navigation actions reuse the existing Home page resource/POM and login actions reuse the existing Login page resource/POM.

 - Guest Home to Login navigation
 - Login completion and authenticated Home return flow
 - Authenticated Home feature-button visibility
 - Authenticated feature URL navigation validation
 - Validate the following exact URL fragments:
 - vehicle-onsite
 - damage-claim
 - presell?data
 - Maintain ownership boundaries between Home, Login, and downstream workflow resources.
 - Generate manual and automation assets using the shared Home page resource/POM.
 
 13. Acceptance Criteria
 
 - Given the user is on the Home page in guest state, when the user selects the Person/Profile button, then the application navigates to the Login page.
 - Given the user is on the Login page, when the user enters approved username and password and selects the Login button, then the application returns to the Home page in authenticated state.
 - Given the authenticated Home page is displayed, when the page loads in authenticated state, then the Vehicles On Site, Damage Claim, and Presell buttons are visible and actionable.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Vehicles On Site button, then the application navigates to a URL containing vehicle-onsite.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Damage Claim button, then the application navigates to a URL containing damage-claim.
 - Given the user is on the Home page in authenticated state, when the user selects the Presell button, then the application navigates to a URL containing presell?data.
 - Given authenticated Home navigation resources are generated, when shared resources are reused, then Home navigation actions reuse the existing Home page resource/POM and login actions reuse the existing Login page resource/POM.
